name: SQLModel
what_it_does: Python ORM that combines SQLAlchemy and Pydantic in a single model definition. SQLModel models serve as both database table definitions (SQLAlchemy) and data validation schemas (Pydantic) simultaneously. Built by the creator of FastAPI to eliminate the duplication of maintaining separate ORM and Pydantic models. Designed for use with FastAPI agent backends.
use_cases: Define agent data models once as SQLModel classes that work as both database tables and FastAPI request/response schemas
Build FastAPI-based agent backends with type-safe database operations and automatic API documentation
Replace separate SQLAlchemy + Pydantic model pairs with a single SQLModel definition in agent APIs
Perform type-safe SQL queries in agent data layers with IDE autocompletion from SQLAlchemy core
Share data models between database, API validation, and agent logic layers without code duplication
not_for: Complex database schemas with many relationships — SQLModel's combined model approach can become unwieldy for complex domain models
Non-FastAPI applications — SQLAlchemy alone is simpler without FastAPI integration needs
Production-critical applications requiring battle-tested stability — SQLModel is still relatively young (0.x versions)
best_when: You're building FastAPI applications and want a single model class that serves as both SQLAlchemy table and Pydantic schema without duplication.
avoid_when: You have a complex domain model separating ORM entities from API schemas, or you need mature production stability — use SQLAlchemy + Pydantic separately.

auth.notes: Local ORM library — authentication handled at application level.

pricing.notes: Free and open source, maintained under the FastAPI organization.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — runs locally against your database

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SQLAlchemy errors surface through SQLModel — IntegrityError, OperationalError with database-specific messages. Pydantic validation errors for schema violations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Standard SQLAlchemy session management. Idempotency is application responsibility.
known_agent_gotchas: SQLModel is still in 0.x — breaking changes have occurred between versions; pin versions carefully in production
table=True models (database tables) vs non-table models (Pydantic schemas) are distinguished by a parameter — easy to confuse when inheriting
Relationship handling in SQLModel differs slightly from raw SQLAlchemy — some advanced relationship patterns require falling back to SQLAlchemy directly
Async support requires SQLAlchemy 2.0 async session — not obvious from the SQLModel documentation which lags behind SQLAlchemy 2.0 changes
Migration support is via Alembic but SQLModel models don't always autogenerate clean migrations — review autogenerated scripts carefully
The SQLModel documentation site is less comprehensive than SQLAlchemy's — complex queries often require consulting SQLAlchemy docs directly

security_score_components.security_notes: Uses SQLAlchemy's parameterized queries — no SQL injection risk. Connection strings must use environment variables, not hardcoded in models.
